content.text: When a player leaves a game for any reason (being expelled out of time or quitting the game), he becomes a "zombie player". In this case, the results of the game won't count for statistics, but we must let the other players finish the game anyway.

Level 0: the passing zombieJust pass to the next step without doing any actionFor example, in 7 wonders, take a random card and discard it (we cannot just pass in this game as it would mess with the next player hand)For example, in Catan, roll the dice and pass
Level 1: the random zombieCode a random possible actionFor example, in 7 wonders, take a random card and play itFor example, in Catan, roll the dice and take a random possible action

Level 2: the greedy zombieTake the action that will bring the most value given the visible information. Isn't planning for future move, nor remember previously known information. Does not try to take a lower value in order to block the opponent.For example, in 7 wonders, take a card that will bring the most points to the players. If there is a tie, the one that will cost less. If there is still a tie, randomly in the subsetFor example, in Catan, roll the dice and play the action that will bring the most points to the players

Level 3: the smart zombieCan plan and/or remember previously revealed information.For example, in 7 wonders, it could remember which cards could be back in the next turn to make the best move.For example, in Catan, instead of declining all trades for zombie, trade only if it's in the benefit of the zombie player
While developing your zombie mode, keep in mind that:

items.text: Do not refer to the rules, because this situation is not planned by the rules.
Try to figure that you are playing with your friends and one of them has to leave: how can we finish the game without killing the spirit of the game?
The idea is NOT to develop an artificial intelligence for the game.
Do not try to end the game early, even in a two-player game. The zombie is there to allow the game to continue, not to end it.

content.text: When you have developed the Zombie Mode, you can indicate the level you made in the Game Metadata Manager. If some parts are one level and some are another level, indicate the predominant one. In case of a tie, indicate the lowest one.
Each time a zombie player must play, the `zombie` function of the state the zombie player is in is called.
Example of Zombie level 1
Example of Zombie level 2

Very important : your zombie code will be called when the player leaves the game. The server will handle the Zombie turn, so there is no current player associated to this action. In your zombieTurn function, you must never use `getCurrentPlayerId()` or `getCurrentPlayerName()` , but use `$playerId` or `$this->getPlayerNameById($playerId)` instead.
Create a table in Friendly mode (the infinite time symbol if you're starting from the game page) and Express Start.
As it's friendly mode, you won't get karma penalty. If it's not friendly mode, you would lose karma, and testing repeatedly, your player might not be allowed to join games anymore!
Click on the debug icon on the top right, select "playToEndRound", and see the bot playing automatically until it reaches the round 2!

heading.text: Implementing the Zombie mode (if you're not using State classes)

content.text: Each time a zombie player must play, your "zombieTurn" method is called.

items.text: $active_player: the id of the active player.

content.text: Most of the time, your zombieTurn method looks like this:
Example of Zombie level 0
Example of Zombie level 1
Example of Zombie level 2

Very important : your zombie code will be called when the player leaves the game. The server will handle the Zombie turn, so there is no current player associated to this action. In your zombieTurn function, you must never use `getCurrentPlayerId()` or `getCurrentPlayerName()` , but use `$active_player` or `$this->getPlayerNameById($active_player)` instead.

heading.text: Testing the Zombie mode (if you're not using State classes)

content.text: Create a table in Friendly mode (the infinite time symbol if you're starting from the game page) and Express Start.
As it's friendly mode, you won't get karma penalty. If it's not friendly mode, you would lose karma, and testing repeatedly, your player might not be allowed to join games anymore!
Click on the debug icon on the top right, select "playToEndRound", and see the bot playing automatically until it reaches the round 2!
